notes.text:
(1) Addiction vs Dependency (biological)
(2) Addiction vs Compulsion (reduce stress / anxiety)
(3) New clinical framework including behavioural addiction (same dopamine spikes, withdrawal, hiding behaviour, relapse)
(4) Social media, internet, phone addiction → normalized behavioural addiction. BUT not a "substance" addiction, as it needs impairment to be clinical. Although it's designed to trigger the same brain networks like gaming.

Open question: what about the subclinical effects on the brain — neural atrophy, decreased real-life activities, social capital, emotional numbness?
